decision: Retain MOM-01. Require separate reporting for: time-series vs cross-sectional momentum; long-leg vs short-leg contribution; large/liquid vs smaller assets; spot long-only vs realistically shortable perpetual implementations; raw vs volatility-managed exposure; ordinary return accounting vs margin/liquidation-aware wealth paths; asset-level concentration and crash attribution.
rationale: Crypto momentum is short-horizon and leg/liquidity dependent; LIT-026 and LIT-027 disagree on crash behavior, so crash attribution and both raw/vol-managed views are mandatory.

decision: Treat CTREND (LIT-024), market-state conditioning, and complex price-volume combinations as later challengers after simple momentum is frozen.
rationale: CTREND is ML-aggregated and not a transparent baseline; promote only after baselines are frozen and realistic controls exist.

decision: Retain LIQ-01 as BOTH a point-in-time eligibility/cost/capacity input AND a separately evaluated candidate return predictor. Require distinguishing liquidity alpha from mechanical universe-selection and cost effects.
rationale: LIT-028 shows liquidity interactions dominate; LIT-029 shows liquidity proxies dominate factor selection. Alpha must be separated from selection/cost.

decision: Split CARRY-01 into separately evaluated mechanisms: (a) fixed-expiry futures basis; (b) perpetual funding; (c) staking rewards; (d) token incentives/emissions if studied later. Do not combine into one generic yield factor without spanning evidence.
rationale: LIT-025 establishes a peer-reviewed crypto carry (futures basis) with stress collapse; LIT-035 covers staking basis; LIT-037 covers funding-rate microstructure. Each needs its own data and test.

decision: Add DIL-01 (Token dilution and unlock pressure) as DEFERRED / UNTESTED. Block empirical testing until point-in-time histories exist for circulating supply, max/FDV supply, unlock schedules, announcement/revision timestamps, actual unlock events, redenominations and token migrations.
rationale: LIT-030 documents a dilution premium in a 2026 working paper; LIT-036 shows unlock negative price relation for ETH. No public point-in-time supply/unlock series located; test gated by data audit.

decision: Retain NET-01 as Phase 2 but raise research priority. Candidate concepts: active addresses / market cap, new-address growth, transaction activity, protocol revenue/fees, MVRV-like measures, network growth, token-function classifications. Do not advance until definitions, provider revisions, chain mappings, spam/entity effects, and availability timestamps are auditable.
rationale: LIT-033 (SHAP) shows new/active addresses among most influential predictors; LIT-036 shows on-chain fundamentals relate to value. Auditability is the binding constraint.

decision: Add robustness requirements against: accepted crypto factors; broad crypto market exposure; volatility and crash exposure; selected traditional-market factors; latent PCA / equivalent hidden-factor controls; token sector / economic-function classifications. Raw long-short returns alone do not establish independent alpha.
rationale: LIT-029 shows 2-3 factors explain all alphas and selections are unstable; LIT-031 shows latent controls materially change premia.

decision: Keep factor interactions, regularized ML, shallow nonlinear models, and information-driven bars as challengers. They may advance only after transparent factor baselines and realistic implementation controls exist.
rationale: LIT-028/LIT-033 show ML/interaction gains but cost/liquidity dependence; LIT-032 is a conflicted practitioner lead. Baselines first.
